original: American explorer Roy Chapman Andrews discovers the first recognized dinosaur eggs in the Gobi Desert, Mongolia
Tom Kenny, American voice actor (SpongeBob), born in Syracuse, New York
Jean-Luc Picard, fictional captain on Star Trek Next Generation, born in La Barre, France

After the Atlantic League-MLB partnership rule changes, Southern Maryland Blue Crabs' outfielder Tony Thomas becomes the first player in professional baseball history to steal first base in a 7-2 win against the Lancaster Barnstormers
Anthony "Spud" Webb, American NBA guard (5f 7inch slam dunk champion), born in Dallas, Texas
World's first championship cat show is organized by Harrison Weir and held at Crystal Palace, London
Live Aid concerts held at both Wembley Stadium (London) and JFK Stadium (Philadelphia, Pennsylvania) raise over $70 million for African famine relief
Outline of 5,000-year-old henge is discovered at Newgrange, Ireland, through drought and drone footage
Babe Ruthhits 700th career home run against Detroit
FIFA World Cup Final, Maracanã Stadium, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil: Mario Götze scores an extra-time winner as Germany beats Argentina 1-0
First football World Cup competition begins in Uruguay
NYC experiences a 25-hour blackout
P. T. Barnum's museum in New York burns down, giantess Anna Haining Swan just escapes
Dean of St. Paul's London, Alexander Nowell, perfects a way to bottle beer
The Hollywood Sign is officially dedicated in the hills above Hollywood, Los Angeles. It originally reads "Hollywoodland," but the last four letters are dropped after renovation in 1949.
Charlotte Dujardin, English equestrian and dressage rider (3x Olympic Gold medals 2012, 2016), born in London, England
Space shuttle STS-70 (Discovery 20) launches
Russia launches unmanned Luna 15 to the Moon
CaptainJames Cookbegins his second voyage to the South Seas aboard HMS Resolution to search for Terra Australis (Southern Continent)
Cigar wins a record 16th consecutive race, tying Citation's record from 1948
Bob Milacki and three other Baltimore Oriole pitchers no-hit A's 2-0
Lamine Yamal, Spanish footballer for Barcelona and Spain, born in Esplugues de Llobregat, Spain
Wyatt Oleff, American actor (It), born in Chicago, Illinois
Source of Mississippi River discovered by American geographerHenry Schoolcraft
Swedish-American inventorJohn Ericssonfiles for a patent for his screw propeller design
US Patent #1 is granted for locomotive wheels after 9,957 unnumbered patents
International Olympic Committee votes to award Beijing the 2008 Summer Olympic Games
RCA releasesElvis Presley's single "Hound Dog," a cover of Big Mama Thornton's original, written by Jerry Leiber and Mike Stoller, backed with "Don't Be Cruel," written by Otis Blackwell
Eddie Van Halenmakes a guest appearance, performing "Beat It" at a Jacksons concert in Dallas, Texas
Unprecedented early fire season in western US as 67 large fires and nearly 918,000 acres are reported burned by Interagency Fire Center
112°F (44°C) is recorded in Mio, Michigan (state record)
114°F (46°C) is recorded in Wisconsin Dells, Wisconsin (state record)
8.5 inches (21.6 cm) of rainfall in Dover, Delaware (state record)
Great Flood: 2,000,000 acres in Kansas and Missouri in central US are flooded by rain-swollen Missouri and Kansas Rivers
Vision of the Virgin Mary appears to children of Fátima, Portugal
FIFA Club World Cup Final: Chelsea defeats Paris Saint-Germain 3–0, becoming the inaugural world champions under the expanded FIFA format
Evian Championship Women's Golf, Evian Resort GC: Australian Grace Kim claims her first major trophy with a miraculous second playoff hole win over Atthaya Thitikul of Thailand
Future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherEarly Wynnwins his 300th and final MLB game at 43
Albin Stenroos wins Olympic marathon (2:41:22.6)
Yadier Molina, Puerto Rican baseball catcher (9 x MLB All Star; World Series 2006, 11; 9 x Gold Glove; St. Louis Cardinals), born in Bayamón, Puerto Rico
Leon Bridges, American soul singer-songwriter ("Coming Home"), born in Atlanta, Georgia
Nathan Law, Hong Kong activist (Umbrella Movement), born in Shenzhen, China
George Nelson "Pinky" American astronaut (STS 41C, STS 61-C, STS-26), born in Charles City, Iowa
Aleksei Yeliseyev, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 5, 8, 10), born in Zhizdra, Soviet Union
Christian Taylor, English filmmaker who wrote for "Star Wars: The Clone Wars" and "Six Feet Under", born in London
Faf du Plessis, South African cricket batsman, captain (69 Tests, 10 x 100, HS 199; 143 ODIs; Northerns CC, Lancashire CCC, Kent CCC + 3 x IPL teams), born in Pretoria, South Africa
Gil Birmingham, Native American-Spanish actor (Yellowstone, Hell or High Water, Wind River), born in San Antonio, Texas
Michael Spinks, American boxer (undisputed world light heavyweight title 1983-85; lineal world heavyweight title 1985-88; Olympic gold middleweight 1976), born in St. Louis, Missouri
Sezen Aksu [Yıldırım], Turkish pop singer-songwriter ("Şımarık" - "Kiss Kiss"), born in Sarayköy, Turkey
Rhonda Vincent, American bluegrass singer, songwriter, and musician, born in Kirksville, Missouri
Simone Veil, French politician (first female President of the European parliament 1979-82), born in Nice, France (d. 2017)
Hubert Reeves, Canadian astrophysicist, and scientific writer, born in Montreal, Quebec (d. 2023)
George Lewis [Joseph Zenon], American jazz clarinetist and bandleader (Bunk Johnson; Preservation Hall Jazz Band; "Burgundy Street Blues"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 1968)
Alfredo M. Santos, Philippine's first four-star General of the Armed Forces, World War II hero, born in Manila (d. 1990)
David Branson, British piano prodigy, composer, musicologist, and visual artist, born in King's Lynn, Norfolk, England (d. 1987)
Hines Johnson, West Indian cricket fast bowler (3 Tests; first WI fast bowler to take 10 wickets in a Test), born in Kingston, Jamaica (d. 1987)
Roger Reynolds, American Pulitzer Prize-winning composer (Whispers Out of Time), born in Detroit, Michigan
Qiu Xigui, Chinese paleographer known for his work on Chinese oracle bones, born in Shanghai, China (d. 2025)
Albert Ayler, American jazz saxophonist, bandleader, and composer (Spititual Unity; The Hilversum Session), born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 1970)
(Jim) "Roger" McGuinn, American folk-rock guitarist, and singer-songwriter (The Byrds - "Turn! Turn! Turn!"; "Eight Miles High"), born in Chicago, Illinois
